species: the lipopolysaccharide-responsive beige-like anchor protein (LRBA) deficiency causes severe autoimmune diseases and cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated protein 4 (CTLA4) loss in humans.
tissue: we documented LRBA and CTLA4 colocalization in human colorectal cancer tissues...
cell_type: we observed a decreased surface and intracellular CTLA4 expression in the activated human CD8+ T cells and CD4+ T cells.
disease_state: LRBA deficiency causes severe autoimmune diseases...
sample_source: A total of 94 human colon tumor tissue samples and 94 para-tumor tissue samples...
anatomic_site_tumor: A total of 94 human colon tumor tissue samples...
cell_line: we confirmed that LRBA bound directly to CTLA4 and regulated its protein stability in human PBMCs and HEK293T cells...
